_disclaimer: State-level policy summary — for reference and UI demonstration. Verify with current legislation before reliance.

04.name: Arizona
04.summary: Transaction privilege tax exemption for qualifying data centers. Major hyperscale destination.

sources.label: Arizona Department of Revenue — Data Center Exemptions

06.name: California
06.summary: Multiple AI transparency laws enacted: AB 2013 (AI training disclosure), AB 2885 (AI definitions), AB 1008 (privacy). SB 1047 (high-risk AI) vetoed by Gov. Newsom in Sep 2024. CCPA/CPRA data privacy rules apply broadly to AI systems.

sources.label: AB 2013 — AI Training Data Transparency Act

08.name: Colorado
08.summary: SB 205 (2024) — Colorado Artificial Intelligence Act: nation's first comprehensive state AI regulation. Requires risk assessments and impact notices for developers and deployers of high-risk AI systems. Signed by Gov. Polis May 2024.

09.name: Connecticut
09.summary: Multiple AI regulation bills proposed 2024–2025 session. Connecticut Data Privacy Act (CTDPA) applies to AI-driven automated decision-making.

13.name: Georgia
13.summary: Sales and use tax exemption on data center equipment and energy. Major growth market for Google, Microsoft, and NCR.

sources.label: Georgia Department of Revenue — Technology Tax Exemptions

17.name: Illinois
17.summary: Artificial Intelligence Video Interview Act (2020): requires disclosure and consent before AI-analyzed video interviews. BIPA biometric data law covers AI facial recognition. Additional AI employment rules.

18.name: Indiana
18.summary: Data center tax exemptions on equipment and electricity for qualified facilities. Major hyperscale hub (Meta, Microsoft, Amazon).

19.name: Iowa
19.summary: 0% sales and use tax on data center equipment and electricity for qualifying facilities (Iowa Code §423.3). Considered the most aggressive state data center incentive package in the US. Google, Microsoft, Facebook all have large campuses.

25.name: Massachusetts
25.summary: Multiple AI oversight bills proposed in 2024–2025 sessions. No comprehensive AI law enacted as of 2025. Consumer protection laws applied to AI systems by AG.

31.name: Nebraska
31.summary: LB 1043 data center investment incentive program. Tax credits for qualifying large-scale data center projects. Low energy costs.

32.name: Nevada
32.summary: Personal property tax abatements and sales tax exemptions for qualifying data centers. Switch Tahoe Reno Industrial Center and other major operators benefit from state incentives.

sources.label: Nevada Governor's Office of Economic Development

36.name: New York
36.summary: NYC Local Law 144 (2023): mandatory bias audits for automated employment decision tools. Additional state-level AI governance legislation proposed. SHIELD Act data privacy rules applicable to AI.

sources.label: NYC Local Law 144 — Automated Employment Decision Tools

37.name: North Carolina
37.summary: Data center investment incentives. Sales and property tax exemptions for qualifying facilities. Apple (Maiden), Google (Lenoir), and other hyperscalers operate major campuses.

39.name: Ohio
39.summary: Data center sales and use tax exemptions on equipment and energy (Ohio R.C. 5739.02). Major AWS, Meta, and Microsoft facilities in the Columbus metro area.

41.name: Oregon
41.summary: Property tax exemptions for qualifying data centers in the Columbia River corridor. Google (The Dalles), Amazon, Apple have major campuses. Note: Hood River County has enacted a municipal data center ban (see county data).

45.name: South Carolina
45.summary: Data center sales tax exemption on equipment and electricity for qualifying investments. Amazon/AWS has major facilities in Berkeley County. State actively courts hyperscale investment.

27.name: Minnesota
27.summary: Minnesota enacted the Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act (MCDPA) in 2024, creating data privacy obligations for AI systems. Minneapolis (Hennepin County) has banned government facial recognition and requires AI impact assessments. Minneapolis-area municipalities have proposed data center moratoriums. Minnesota does not have a statewide data center tax incentive as of 2025.
sources: Minneapolis City Council facial recognition ban (2021)

47.name: Tennessee
47.summary: Sales tax exemption on data center equipment for qualifying facilities (Tenn. Code Ann. § 67-6-206). xAI Colossus supercomputer facility (world's largest GPU cluster as of 2024) operates in Memphis (Shelby County). Multiple TN counties enacted data center moratoriums in 2024 citing TVA grid capacity concerns.

sources.label: Tennessee Department of Revenue — Data Center Exemption

48.name: Texas
48.summary: No state income tax. Chapter 403 successor incentive programs for large capital projects. No state AI regulation enacted as of 2025. One of the largest US data center markets.

51.name: Virginia
51.summary: Largest US data center market; Northern Virginia is the world's largest data center hub (70%+ of global internet traffic). Virginia Data Center Incentive Package includes sales tax exemptions on equipment and software. Note: individual counties (e.g. Loudoun) have significant local zoning restrictions — see county data.

sources.label: Virginia DHCD — Data Center Incentive Package
NVTC — Northern Virginia Tech Council Data Center Report

53.name: Washington
53.summary: Governor's Executive Order 23-03 on AI (2023). SHB 1951 (2024): requires state agencies to inventory AI systems. Multiple additional AI governance bills enacted. Growing data center market in the Columbia Basin.

sources.label: WA SHB 1951 — AI Accountability in Government (2024)

22.name: Louisiana
22.summary: Industrial Tax Exemption Program (ITEP) provides up to 100% property tax exemptions for 10 years for qualifying industrial facilities including data centers. No state income tax on manufacturing equipment. Meta announced a major data center in Richland Parish ($800M+) in 2023.

sources.label: Meta Richland Parish Louisiana Data Center

26.name: Michigan
26.summary: Michigan has proposed AI governance legislation in 2024–2025 sessions including data privacy bills. Several counties (Kalamazoo, Washtenaw) have proposed data center moratoriums as the state weighs large-scale data center incentive packages. Michigan does not have a statewide data center tax exemption as of 2025.

sources.label: Michigan Legislature — AI and Data Privacy Bills

44.name: Rhode Island
44.summary: Rhode Island has no statewide data center incentive program. The Town of Smithfield (Providence County) enacted a permanent ban on large data centers in 2023 — one of the first US municipalities to do so — citing energy grid, water, and community impacts. This ban has been cited as a model by municipalities in other states.

sources.label: Smithfield RI data center ban — Data Center Bans

55.name: Wisconsin
55.summary: Data center property tax exemption for qualifying facilities. Microsoft announced a $3.3B, 1GW+ AI data center campus in Mount Pleasant (Racine County) in May 2024 — one of the largest tech investments in Wisconsin history. Some counties (Dane, Manitowoc) have proposed local oversight requirements.

sources.label: Microsoft Mount Pleasant Wisconsin AI Campus

56.name: Wyoming
56.summary: Low energy costs, no state income tax, minimal regulation. Growing destination for crypto mining and data centers. Supports high-performance computing.
